title: Queen's Gambit
shortDescription: A classical 1.d4 d5 2.c4 opening, offering a wing pawn to gain central space and a lead in development.

children.comment: The Queen's Gambit challenges Black's d5 pawn.
Black declines the gambit and reinforces d5.
White increases pressure on d5.
The bishop pins the knight and increases pressure on the center.
White develops solidly and prepares Nf3 and Rc1.
The rook supports c-file pressure and possible cxd5 ideas.

line.name: Queen's Gambit Declined: Orthodox Setup
line.description: The classical QGD structure with Nc3, Nf3, Bg5 and e3.

line.name: QGD Orthodox: Main Line
line.description: A classical main line with Rc1, Bd3 and O-O.

line.name: QGD Orthodox: Deep Main Line
line.description: A deeper Orthodox line featuring the classic central tension.

children.comment: White fixes the pawn structure and prepares a minority attack.

line.name: Queen's Gambit Declined: Exchange Variation
line.description: White exchanges on d5 and reaches the Carlsbad structure.

children.comment: White prepares the thematic minority attack.

line.name: QGD Exchange: Minority Attack
line.description: White uses Rb1 and b4-b5 to attack Black's queenside pawn structure.

children.comment: White prepares the thematic e4 break.

line.name: QGD Exchange: Kingside Attack
line.description: White uses Nge2, f3 and e4 to build a central and kingside attack.

line.name: Semi-Tarrasch Defense
line.description: White reaches an isolated-queen-pawn structure with active piece play.

line.name: Ragozin Defense
line.description: White meets ...Bb4 with e3 and Qc2, maintaining central pressure.

children.comment: White builds a powerful center.

line.name: Vienna Variation
line.description: White enters a sharp QGD line with Nc3 and Nf3 before Bg5.

children.comment: The Tarrasch Defense attacks White's center immediately.
White prepares a kingside fianchetto and pressure on d5.

line.name: Tarrasch Defense: Main Setup
line.description: Black challenges the center with ...c5; White develops and pressures d5.

line.name: Tarrasch Defense: Main Line
line.description: White targets the isolated d-pawn and develops harmoniously.

line.name: Tarrasch: Schlechter-Rubinstein Setup
line.description: White uses g3 and Bg2 to pressure the isolated d-pawn.

children.comment: White heads for a Catalan-style structure.

line.name: QGD with Catalan Setup
line.description: White fianchettos the bishop and pressures the long diagonal.

children.comment: Black accepts the gambit pawn.
White prepares Bxc4 and rapid development.
White regains the pawn and develops with tempo.

line.name: Queen's Gambit Accepted: 3.e3
line.description: White prepares to regain the c4 pawn with Bxc4.

children.comment: White develops and prepares e3.

line.name: Queen's Gambit Accepted: 3.Nf3
line.description: A flexible line where White delays e3 and prevents ...e5 ideas.

children.comment: White grabs central space instead of immediately recovering the pawn.

line.name: QGA: Central Expansion with e4
line.description: White builds a broad center after Black accepts the pawn.

line.name: QGA: 3.e4 Main Line
line.description: An ambitious line where White creates a strong pawn center.

line.name: QGA 3.e4: Deep Main Line
line.description: White accepts an isolated structure in return for activity and initiative.

children.comment: White prevents ...b5 from safely holding the extra pawn.

line.name: Slav Defense: Main Setup
line.description: White develops naturally against Black's solid ...c6 structure.

line.name: Slav Main Line with a4
line.description: White prevents ...b5 and prepares to regain the c4 pawn.

children.comment: The Geller Gambit: White builds a huge center.

line.name: Slav: Geller Gambit
line.description: White sacrifices a pawn for rapid development and central initiative.

children.comment: White supports d4 and prepares Bd3.

line.name: Semi-Slav: Basic Development
line.description: White enters the main Semi-Slav structure with e3 and Nf3.

line.name: Semi-Slav: Meran Setup
line.description: White develops Bd3 and prepares e4 after Black captures on c4.

children.comment: White avoids immediate Meran theory and supports e4.

line.name: Semi-Slav: Anti-Meran with Qc2
line.description: White delays Bd3 and keeps more flexibility.

children.comment: White enters the Anti-Meran complex.

line.name: Semi-Slav: Botvinnik Variation
line.description: One of the sharpest branches in all of opening theory.

children.comment: White damages Black's structure and sidesteps the Botvinnik.

line.name: Semi-Slav: Moscow Variation
line.description: White gives up the bishop pair to avoid the sharpest Botvinnik lines.

line.name: Semi-Slav: Anti-Moscow Gambit
line.description: White keeps the bishop and enters a very sharp kingside struggle.

children.comment: White chooses the Exchange Slav.

line.name: Slav Exchange Variation
line.description: White reaches a symmetrical structure but can still play for pressure.

children.comment: Black offers the Albin Countergambit.
Black gains space and cramps White's development.
White attacks the d4 pawn and develops.
White prevents ...Bb4+ and prepares b4 ideas.

line.name: Albin Countergambit: Safe Response
line.description: White accepts Black's countergambit and challenges the advanced pawn.

line.name: Albin Countergambit: Main Line
line.description: White challenges the advanced d4 pawn and develops safely.

line.name: Albin Countergambit: Avoiding the Lasker Trap
line.description: White uses a safe development scheme and avoids the famous tactical trap.

children.comment: White clarifies the center.

line.name: Chigorin Defense: Basic Line
line.description: White builds a broad center against Black's early ...Nc6.

line.name: Chigorin Defense: Main Line
line.description: White uses cxd5 and Nc3 to exploit Black's unusual development.

line.name: Chigorin Defense: Tactical Main Line
line.description: A forcing branch where White accepts structural damage for the bishop pair and center.

children.comment: White exploits Black's lack of support for d5.
White gains a strong center and attacks the knight.

line.name: Marshall Defense
line.description: White gains space against the passive ...Nf6 setup.

line.name: Baltic Defense
line.description: White challenges Black's early bishop development with cxd5 and Nc3.
